title: Other Fees

rows.due_date: On or before the Friday following the end of the preceding week

rows.amount: Not to exceed 0.75% of gross sales
rows.due_date: On or before the Friday following the end of the preceding week

rows.amount: Not to exceed 3 1/2% of gross sales
rows.due_date: On or before the Friday following the end of the preceding week or within 90 days following the end of the week

rows.amount: 50% of the then-current initial franchise fee charged

rows.due_date: Subsequent to the opening of the store

rows.amount: Maximum by law or 1 1/2% per month
rows.due_date: From date payment is due until paid in full
From date payment is due until paid in full

rows.amount: Actual cost of audit plus interest on overdue amount

table_notes: With the exception of local advertising, refurbishing, and insurance premiums, all the fees listed above are imposed by and payable to RFC. The fees are non-refundable and are uniformly imposed.

Note 1: Royalty is 5% weekly. Retroactively reduced to 3.5% if annual sales below $425,000 (52-week year) or $433,173 (53-week year). RFC refunds excess within 30 days of fiscal year-end. Must be current on all fees to receive refund.
Note 2: Marketing fund fee of 0.75% of gross sales payable weekly.
Note 3: Local advertising tiered: 1.5% (no electronic media), 2.5% (radio), 3.5% (TV). Increase requires 60% franchisee approval. Local Ad Fund not currently being collected.
Note 4: Transfer fee is 50% of then-current initial franchise fee. Covers credit checks, attorney fees, and training for new franchisee.
Note 5: Additional training costs (transport, lodging, meals) borne by franchisee.
Note 6: Interest at maximum rate by law or 1.5% per month on overdue payments.
Note 7: Franchisee must indemnify and hold RFC harmless for broad categories of claims.
Note 8: If audit shows deficiency exceeding 2% of reported gross sales, franchisee pays all audit costs plus deficiency plus interest.

title: Your Estimated Initial Investment
column_headers: To Whom Payment Is To Be Made

rows.method: If FDA signed: 2 installments of $12,500. If not: 1 installment of $25,000

table_notes: Note 1: See Item 5 for additional info on initial franchise fee.
Note 2: Equipment includes computer systems, phones, ovens, dishes, cooking utensils.

Note 3: Locations may be enclosed malls, food courts, strip-center malls, storefronts, free-standing buildings. Leasehold costs vary by size, location, material, labor. Free-standing costs substantially higher than projected. Mall ~900 sq ft, strip center ~2,800 sq ft, free-standing ~3,800 sq ft.
Note 4: Opening inventory covers ~2-3 weeks of operation.
Note 5: Security deposits may be refundable. May not be required at landlord/utility discretion.
Note 6: Promotion costs vary by media type and location.
Note 7: Pre-opening training covers ~1 week of training new employees.
Note 8: Includes attorney's fees and permit costs.
Note 9: Additional funds estimate for 3 months based on affiliate experience. Actual needs vary by territory size, time of year, management skill, economic conditions, competition.
Note 10: Annual business owner's policy covering liability, property damage, loss of income, loss of property.

Estimate is for owner-operated business, does not include salaries/benefits for employees or owner's draw. RFC estimates additional cash needed during first 3-9 months or longer. Payments to RFC are non-refundable. RFC does not offer direct or indirect financing.

rows.obligation: j. Warranty and customer service requirements
k. Territorial development and sales quota
m. Maintenance, appearance, and remodeling requirements
y. Other (covenants, hours, taxes, permits, indebtedness)

title: Training Program
table_notes: Training at RFC's training store in Mayfair Mall, Wauwatosa, Wisconsin.
10 weeks, 5 business days per week, 8-10 hours per day.
All training is on-the-job; 0 classroom hours for all subjects.
Training conducted by RFC staff with at least 1 year in-field experience.
Additional on-the-job training available at affiliate restaurants for up to 20 weeks (not required, recommended).
Opening assistance: 5 business days (3 days before scheduled opening).
Initial training program is mandatory. Failure to complete to RFC's satisfaction is grounds for termination.
Franchisee bears costs of transportation, lodging, meals, wages, and employee benefits for training.

title: Renewal, Termination, Transfer, and Dispute Resolution — The Franchise Relationship
column_headers: Section in Franchise or Other Agreement

rows.summary: 15 years (FA); 180 days (FDA)
One additional 15-year renewal if in good standing (FA); as agreed (FDA)
Give notice, execute then-current FA (may have materially different terms), general release, remodel, retraining. No renewal fee.
No provision in FA. FDA: may terminate if unable to acquire site within 180 days.

rows.provision: e. Termination by franchisor without cause
rows.summary: No provision in FA. FDA: may terminate if unable to acquire site within 180 days.

rows.provision: f. Termination by franchisor with cause
rows.summary: RFC can terminate only if franchisee is in default.
60 days to cure: fail to submit reports, bankruptcy, property attached, goodwill impairment, fail to comply, fail to maintain insurance, etc. 10 days to cure non-payment of fees.

Adjudged bankrupt, assignment for creditors, abandonment, criminal misconduct, material misrepresentation, loss of possession, unauthorized assignment/disclosure, intentional understatement of gross sales, repeated non-compliance.
Payment of amounts due, complete de-identification, covenant not to compete.
No restriction on RFC's right to assign.
Includes transfer of contract, assets, or ownership change.
RFC has right to approve all transfers; will not unreasonably withhold.

rows.provision: m. Conditions for approval of transfer
rows.summary: New franchisee qualifies, accounts current, transfer fee paid, training arranged, release signed, noncompete signed, remodel, current agreement signed by new franchisee.
RFC can match any offer for the franchise business.
No standalone option.
Franchise must be assigned by estate to approved buyer or heir within 6 months.
No involvement in competing business anywhere. Subject to state law.
FA: 1 year within 5 miles of designated area or 5 miles of any system location. FDA: 1 year within 50 miles.
No modifications, but Operations Manual subject to change.
Only FA terms, exhibits and signed agreements enforceable. Does not disclaim express FDD representations.
Binding arbitration via AAA at its office nearest RFC. WI Franchise Investment Law and Fair Dealership Act legality not subject to arbitration.
No restriction on choice of forum. Subject to state law.
Wisconsin law applies. Subject to state law.

table_notes: Either party may request binding arbitration. Legality of WI Franchise Investment Law and WI Fair Dealership Act are not subject to arbitration.
FA and FDA have differing noncompete radii: FA uses 5 miles, FDA uses 50 miles.

title: Statements of Operations and Retained Earnings — FY2024, FY2023, FY2022
table_notes: Revenue growth of 53.6% from FY2022 to FY2024 driven by franchised unit expansion and royalty growth.
Transfer fees of $50,000 in FY2024 consistent with 1 transfer at roughly 50% of franchise fee plus possible predecessor conversions.
SGA expenses include related-party management/consulting fees of $167,977 (2024), $137,905 (2023), $99,874 (2022).

title: Cooperative Advertising Fund Activity
table_notes: From Note 2 to financial statements.
Fund spending exceeded contributions in FY2024, drawing down the surplus.
Affiliate locations contributed $59,194 (2024), $58,516 (2023), $60,238 (2022) to the fund.
Amounts due back to affiliates included in surplus: $24,901 (2024), $36,754 (2023), $48,860 (2022).

title: Location Information — Notes to Financial Statements (Note 3)
table_notes: There was one additional location which was transferred between franchisees during 2023.
